feedback_addressed: Added BLK SELL recommendation with 32% above 10-year median PE analysis and unproven private markets strategy critique
Consolidated V and MA into V only (18%) with explicit justification: lower forward PE, larger international mix, CCCA insulation
Added comprehensive recession stress test for every pick (base/mild/severe EPS scenarios with specific numbers)
Quantified CCCA impact: $4-5.5B revenue at risk for Visa (10-14% of total), 25-30% probability of passage
Added credit cycle analysis with current delinquency rates, charge-off rates, historical averages, and EPS sensitivity per 100bp
Added deposit beta and NIM compression analysis for JPM ($96-98B NII downside in 4-cut scenario)
Added Basel III endgame impact analysis (scaled back, net positive for banks)
Added Citigroup as contrarian pick at 0.7x TBV replacing MA -- directly responsive to judge demand for value opportunity
Upgraded PGR from HOLD to BUY with insurance cycle analysis including 40% ROE justification and mean-reversion risk
Added position sizing with entry/exit criteria, stop-losses, and risk-reward ratios for every pick
Added M&A reversion analysis: if volumes return to 2018-2019 levels, GS EPS drops to $38-42
Bank PE framework clarified: P/TBV and ROTCE as proper valuation metrics instead of comparing to tech multiples
remaining_flaws: JPM CEO succession risk is mentioned but lacks the historical precedent quantification the v1 critique demanded (stock performance post-iconic-CEO departure)
MS negative FCF analysis is thorough but the -$37B 2023 figure needs more context on whether this is truly comparable to operating cash flow negative
GS weight reduced from 18% to 15% but still represents second-largest position -- in a true M&A reversion, this position would be deeply underwater at 15%
what_impressed: The V vs MA consolidation with specific metrics (23.5x vs 25.5x forward PE, 45% vs 35% international revenue) is exactly what was demanded and executed cleanly
Citigroup addition as a 0.7x TBV contrarian play with 2.3:1 risk-reward ratio demonstrates the analyst can find value, not just momentum
Credit cycle analysis with small bank delinquency at 6.6% as a leading indicator for large bank stress is genuine analytical insight
Recession stress tests with specific EPS numbers (GS from $58 to $22 in severe recession, referencing 2022 precedent) provide actionable risk management
PGR insurance cycle analysis with combined ratio reversion from 84% toward 90-93% and upgrade rationale despite the cycle turning shows nuanced thinking
CCCA probability-weighted analysis with specific dollar impact and Durbin Amendment historical precedent provides the regulatory scenario analysis v1 lacked
